title: S & SH Sounds
focus: Crisp, clean S and SH - don't let them blur into each other.
twisters: She sells seashells by the seashore.
Six sleek swans swam swiftly southwards.
The sixth sick sheikh's sixth sheep's sick.
Shy Shelly says she should share her sushi.

title: R & L Sounds
focus: A common mix-up - keep R and L clearly separate, especially at speed.
twisters: Red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry.
Really leery, rarely Larry.
The lazy lion roared loudly near the river.
Round the rugged rock the ragged rascal ran.

title: TH Sounds
focus: Put your tongue between your teeth for TH - don't swap it for T or D.
twisters: Think of three thin things.
That thing on the third shelf belongs to them.
The thirty-three thieves thought they thrilled the throne.
This thick thread is thoroughly thin.

title: P & B Sounds
focus: P is a sharp puff of air, B is a soft vibration - feel the difference.
twisters: Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.
Betty bought a bit of butter, but the butter was bitter.
A big bug bit a big black bear.
Please pack up the plastic pickup packet.

title: V & W Sounds
focus: One of the trickiest for English learners - V touches your teeth, W doesn't.
twisters: We wondered whether the weather would worsen.
Vivian's video went viral very quickly.
Wendy's wolf watched Vincent's van vanish.
Very well, we will visit the village on Wednesday.

title: CH & J Sounds
focus: CH is a sharp, unvoiced sound; J is soft and voiced - keep them distinct.
twisters: Chop shops stock chops.
Chesty chicken chewed cheese chunks cheerfully.
Jenny's giant jelly jiggled joyfully.
Which witch switched the Swiss wristwatches?

title: Fast K & G Sounds
focus: Sharp, quick consonants - great for building overall speaking speed.
twisters: A big black bug bit a big black bear.
Can you can a can as a canner can can a can?
Greek grapes, Greek grapes, Greek grapes.
Kindly keep the King's key in the kitchen.

title: Numbers & Multi-Syllable Words
focus: Long words trip people up - break them into syllables first, then speed up.
twisters: Thirty-three thieves thought they thrilled the throne throughout Thursday.
Eleven benevolent elephants elegantly ambled.
The statistical analysis required particularly meticulous methodology.
Unquestionably, the entrepreneurship opportunity was extraordinarily lucrative.

title: Tricky IT & Business Words
focus: The exact words that trip people up in real meetings - worth over-practicing.
twisters: Authentication, infrastructure, vulnerability, asynchronous.
The algorithm's architecture requires careful reconfiguration.
Specifically, particularly, comfortably, thoroughly.
The entrepreneur's collaborative infrastructure strategy was extraordinarily comprehensive.

title: Mixed Rapid-Fire Challenge
focus: Everything at once - the real test of clear, confident articulation.
twisters: How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?
Fuzzy Wuzzy was a bear, Fuzzy Wuzzy had no hair.
I scream, you scream, we all scream for ice cream.
She sells seashells, Peter Piper picked pickled peppers, and the sixth sick sheikh's sheep is sick.
